g.text: Total: 64 bytes = 1 cache line — mutex AND counter share it
Every lock() write INVALIDATES counter on ALL other cores
M (Modified) | dirty, exclusive — only this core has it
E (Exclusive) | clean, exclusive — only this core has it
I (Invalid) | stale — must fetch before use
RFO Request | core sends 'I want ownership' → all others: I
Cost per RFO | ~100ns (L3 hit) or ~300ns (NUMA remote)
Synchronization Cost Comparison (16 threads, 1000 acq/sec each)
Approach | Cache Lines | Cost/op | Total overhead/sec
FIX 1: atomic only | 1 bouncing | ~50ns | 0.8ms (no mutex, just XADD)
FIX 2: per-thread | N private | ~1ns | 0.016ms (pure local write)
Mutex alone (padded)| 1 bouncing | ~150ns | 2.4ms (MESI only on lock line)
MESI State: bounces M→I→M→I on every lock/unlock cycle
Ping-pong cost: ~100–300ns per ownership transfer (RFO + invalidate)
At 16,000 acq/sec: 1.6–4.8ms/sec of pure coherence overhead
Separate mutex and counter onto different cache lines
Now: lock ping-pong on line 0 ONLY
Counter updates: LOCK XADD — single bus transaction, no mutex
Per-thread local counter — zero contention on hot path
0x00 | int | local_count // private to this thread
0x04 | char | _pad[60] // pad to 64B — OWN cache line
Read path: sum all local_count values under one mutex
Hot path: zero synchronization, zero cache contention

text.tspan: // No mutex needed for the counter itself
// x86 compiles to: LOCK XADD [mem], reg
// Still causes cache line bouncing, but
// No mutex needed for the counter itself
// x86 compiles to: LOCK XADD [mem], reg
// Still causes cache line bouncing, but
// Hot path: thread writes only its OWN cache line
// No lock, no atomic, no cross-core traffic
// Hot path: thread writes only its OWN cache line
// No lock, no atomic, no cross-core traffic
